revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | Group = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 41 | Latitude Seconds = 51.45 | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 48 | Longitude Seconds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata |ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Uwanabe Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uwanabe Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uwanabe Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Uwanabe Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uwanabe Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uwanabe Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Uwanabe Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Uwanabe Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:ウワナベ古墳]]

The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata |ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Uwanabe Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoen-fun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uwanabe Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uwanabe Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Uwanabe Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uwanabe Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uwanabe Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Uwanabe Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Uwanabe Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Uwanabe Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:ウワナベ古墳]]

It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata |ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Uwanabe Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uwanabe Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uwanabe Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Uwanabe Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uwanabe Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uwanabe Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Uwanabe Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Uwanabe Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Uwanabe Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:ウワナベ古墳]]

This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Uwanabe Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uwanabe Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uwanabe Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Uwanabe Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uwanabe Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uwanabe Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Uwanabe Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Uwanabe Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Uwanabe Kofun]][[ja:ウワナベ古墳]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | Group = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 41 | Latitude Seconds = 51.45 | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 48 | Longitude Seconds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata |ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Uwanabe Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uwanabe Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Uwanabe Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uwanabe Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Uwanabe Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uwanabe Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Uwanabe Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uwanabe Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Uwanabe Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Uwanabe Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Uwanabe Kofun]][[ja:ウワナベ古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | Group = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 41 | Latitude Seconds = 51.45 | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 48 | Longitude Seconds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata |ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Uwanabe Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | Group = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 41 | Latitude Seconds = 51.45 | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 48 | Longitude Seconds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata |ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | Group = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 41 | Latitude Seconds = 51.45 | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 48 | Longitude Seconds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata |ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Uwanabe Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | Group = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 41 | Latitude Seconds = 51.45 | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 48 | Longitude Seconds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata |ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Uwanabe Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | Group = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 41 | Latitude Seconds = 51.45 | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 48 | Longitude Seconds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata |ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Uwanabe Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | Group = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 41 | Latitude Seconds = 51.45 | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 48 | Longitude Seconds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata |ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Uwanabe Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | Group = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 41 | Latitude Seconds = 51.45 | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 48 | Longitude Seconds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata |ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Uwanabe Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | Group = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 41 | Latitude Seconds = 51.45 | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 48 | Longitude Seconds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata |ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | Group = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 41 | Latitude Seconds = 51.45 | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 48 | Longitude Seconds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | Type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata |ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} revision.comment: linking [[Zenpokoenfun]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | Group = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 41 | Latitude Seconds = 51.45 | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 48 | Longitude Seconds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Size = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata |ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Uwanabe Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata |ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | Group = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 41 | Latitude Seconds = 51.45 | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 48 | Longitude Seconds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Size = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata |ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Uwanabe Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Uwanabe Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]
