revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | Group = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 41 | Latitude Seconds = 51.45 | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 48 | Longitude Seconds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Size = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata |ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | Group = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 41 | Latitude Seconds = 51.45 | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 48 | Longitude Seconds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Size = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata |ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata |ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | Group = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 41 | Latitude Seconds = 51.45 | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 48 | Longitude Seconds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Size = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata |ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | Group = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 41 | Latitude Seconds = 51.45 | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 48 | Longitude Seconds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Size = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata |ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata |ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} revision.comment: /* Structure and Size */
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | Group = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 41 | Latitude Seconds = 51.45 | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 48 | Longitude Seconds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Size = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata|ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable burial mounds such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | Group = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 41 | Latitude Seconds = 51.45 | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 48 | Longitude Seconds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Size = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata|ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | Group = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 41 | Latitude Seconds = 51.45 | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 48 | Longitude Seconds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Size = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata|ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata|ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | Group = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 41 | Latitude Seconds = 51.45 | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 48 | Longitude Seconds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Size = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata|ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | Group = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 41 | Latitude Seconds = 51.45 | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 48 | Longitude Seconds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Size = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]

revision.comment: moving the kofun template to the bottom of each page
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | Group = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 41 | Latitude Seconds = 51.45 | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 48 | Longitude Seconds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Size = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata|ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Uwanabe Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} revision.comment: puttin it at the bottom too ant not just in the comment
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | Group = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 41 | Latitude Seconds = 51.45 | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 48 | Longitude Seconds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Size = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata|ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | Group = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 41 | Latitude Seconds = 51.45 | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 48 | Longitude Seconds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Size = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata|ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and Size == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} revision.comment: another mass edit to try to fix parameters
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | Group = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates Degrees = 34 | coordinates Minutes = 41 | coordinates Seconds = 51.45 | coordinates Degrees = 135 | coordinates Minutes = 48 | coordinates Seconds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata|ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and area == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Uwanabe Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinatesrees = 34 | coordinatesutes = 41 | coordinatesonds = 51.45 | coordinatesrees = 135 | coordinatesutes = 48 | coordinatesonds = 32.47 | ISO = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | Period = Mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = {{ill|Princess Hata|ja|八田皇女}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe Burial Reference Site" | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | Notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. Built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata|ja|八田皇女}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and area == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key dimensions: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Uwanabe Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}}
