Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 41 | coordinates = 51.45 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 32.47 | omitted = JP-29 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | built = Mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]] | burials = [[:en:Princess Yata|Princess Hata]] (proposed by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) | burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe burial Reference Site" | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size Rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=41|coordinates=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|map_dot_label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the burial site of [[:en:Princess Yata|Princess Yata]], daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and consort of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. == burial and area == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]. The first tier was surrounded by a row of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|10=ja|11=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|comment=trying to get the bot to work with these using this|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|qq=}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha-jinja Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key area: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flanged cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} spans the northernmost edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konabe Kofun|qq=}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniba Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniba Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Uwanabe Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|ウワナベ古墳|version=102699180|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Uwanabe Kofun]][[sn:Uwanabe Kofun]]}} [[sn:Uwanabe Kofun]] [[en:Uwanabe Kofun]] [[ja:ウワナベ古墳]] [[sn:wanabe Kofun]] [[Category:佐保佐紀路]] [[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]] [[Category:奈良市の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Candidate Imperial Mausoleums]] {{Commons category|Uwanabe Kofun}}

It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konabe Kofun|qq=}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniba Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniba Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Uwanabe Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|ウワナベ古墳|version=102699180|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Uwanabe Kofun]][[sn:Uwanabe Kofun]]}} [[sn:Uwanabe Kofun]] [[ja:ウワナベ古墳]] [[sn:wanabe Kofun]] [[Category:佐保佐紀路]] [[Category:佐紀盾列古墳群]] [[Category:奈良市の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Uwanabe Kofun}}

revision.comment: Bot: update category → Saki Tatenami Kofun Group
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 41 | coordinates = 51.45 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 32.47 | omitted = JP-29 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | built = Mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]] | burials = [[:en:Princess Yata|Princess Hata]] (proposed by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) | burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe burial Reference Site" | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size Rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=41|coordinates=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|map_dot_label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the burial site of [[:en:Princess Yata|Princess Yata]], daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and consort of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. == burial and area == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]. The first tier was surrounded by a row of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|10=ja|11=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|comment=trying to get the bot to work with these using this|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|qq=}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha-jinja Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key area: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flanged cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} spans the northernmost edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konabe Kofun|qq=}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniba Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniba Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Uwanabe Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|ウワナベ古墳|version=102699180|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Uwanabe Kofun]][[sn:Uwanabe Kofun]]}} [[sn:Uwanabe Kofun]] [[ja:ウワナベ古墳]] [[sn:wanabe Kofun]] [[Category:佐保佐紀路]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group�]] [[Category:奈良市の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Uwanabe Kofun}}

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 41 | coordinates = 51.45 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 32.47 | omitted = JP-29 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | built = Mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]] | burials = [[:en:Princess Yata|Princess Hata]] (proposed by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) | burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe burial Reference Site" | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size Rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=41|coordinates=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|map_dot_label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the burial site of [[:en:Princess Yata|Princess Yata]], daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and consort of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. == burial and area == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]. The first tier was surrounded by a row of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|10=ja|11=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|comment=trying to get the bot to work with these using this|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|qq=}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha-jinja Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key area: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flanged cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} spans the northernmost edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konabe Kofun|qq=}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniba Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniba Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Uwanabe Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|ウワナベ古墳|version=102699180|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Uwanabe Kofun]][[sn:Uwanabe Kofun]]}} [[sn:Uwanabe Kofun]] [[ja:ウワナベ古墳]] [[sn:wanabe Kofun]] [[Category:佐保佐紀路]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:History of Nara�]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Uwanabe Kofun}}

Key area: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flanged cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} spans the northernmost edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konabe Kofun|qq=}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniba Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniba Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Uwanabe Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|ウワナベ古墳|version=102699180|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Uwanabe Kofun]][[sn:Uwanabe Kofun]]}} [[sn:Uwanabe Kofun]] [[ja:ウワナベ古墳]] [[sn:wanabe Kofun]] [[Category:佐保佐紀路]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Uwanabe Kofun}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Fixed Kofun templates
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 41 | coordinates = 51.45 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 32.47 | omitted = JP-29 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | built = Mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]] | burials = [[:en:Princess Yata|Princess Hata]] (proposed by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) | burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe burial Reference Site" | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size Rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=41|coordinates=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|map_dot_label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the burial site of [[:en:Princess Yata|Princess Yata]], daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and consort of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. == burial and area == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]. The first tier was surrounded by a row of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]], Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha-jinja Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key area: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flanged cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} spans the northernmost edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konabe Kofun|qq=}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniba Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniba Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Uwanabe Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|ウワナベ古墳|version=102699180|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Uwanabe Kofun]][[sn:Uwanabe Kofun]]}} [[sn:Uwanabe Kofun]] [[ja:ウワナベ古墳]] [[sn:wanabe Kofun]] [[Category:佐保佐紀路]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Uwanabe Kofun}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 41 | coordinates = 51.45 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 32.47 | omitted = JP-29 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | built = Mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]] | burials = [[:en:Princess Yata|Princess Hata]] (proposed by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) | burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe burial Reference Site" | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size Rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=41|coordinates=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|map_dot_label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the burial site of [[:en:Princess Yata|Princess Yata]], daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and consort of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. == burial and area == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]. The first tier was surrounded by a row of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]], Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha-jinja Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key area: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flanged cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} spans the northernmost edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konabe Kofun|qq=}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniba Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniba Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Uwanabe Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|ウワナベ古墳|version=102699180|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Uwanabe Kofun]][[sn:Uwanabe Kofun]]}} [[sn:Uwanabe Kofun]] [[ja:ウワナベ古墳]] [[sn:wanabe Kofun]] [[Category:佐保佐紀路]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Uwanabe Kofun}}

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:佐保佐紀路]] into [[Category:Historic Saho-Saki Road]] via bot
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Uwanabe Kofun | Alternative name = 宇和奈辺古墳 | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 41 | coordinates = 51.45 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 32.47 | omitted = JP-29 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Length 270-280 meters{{cite web|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20201120/k00/00m/040/291000c|title="Uwanabe" reaches 280 meters in length; the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}, Height 19.8 meters | built = Mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]] | burials = [[:en:Princess Yata|Princess Hata]] (proposed by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) | burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe burial Reference Site" | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} | image = [[File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
The full view of the tumulus (front section on the left, rear circular section in the right background) | notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size Rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13. | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=41|coordinates=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|map_dot_label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} }} == Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the burial site of [[:en:Princess Yata|Princess Yata]], daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and consort of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. == burial and area == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]. The first tier was surrounded by a row of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus. Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]], Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha-jinja Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan. Key area: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front) File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the rear File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the west File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flanged cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] == Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} spans the northernmost edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konabe Kofun|qq=}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniba Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniba Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査抄報|publisher=奈良県|year=1949}} * {{Cite book|和書|doi=10.24484/sitereports.130938|title=奈良市埋蔵文化財調査年報|publisher=Nara City Board of Education|year=2023|chapter=ウワナベ古墳の調査 UN第2次}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Uwanabe Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|ウワナベ古墳|version=102699180|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Uwanabe Kofun]][[sn:Uwanabe Kofun]]}} [[sn:Uwanabe Kofun]] [[ja:ウワナベ古墳]] [[sn:wanabe Kofun]] [[Category:Historic Saho-Saki Road]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Uwanabe Kofun}}

== Overview ==
The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the burial site of [[:en:Princess Yata|Princess Yata]], daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and consort of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].

== burial and area ==
The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]. The first tier was surrounded by a row of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus.

Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]], Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha-jinja Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan.<ref name="Mainichi20201121" />

Key area:
* Front section width: 129.5 meters
* Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters
* Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front)

<gallery>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the east</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view of the front section</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the rear</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the west</small>
File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flanged cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]]
</gallery>

== Geography ==
The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} spans the northernmost edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konabe Kofun|qq=}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment.

== Overview ==
The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the burial site of [[:en:Princess Yata|Princess Yata]], daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and consort of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].

== burial and area ==
The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]. The first tier was surrounded by a row of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus.

Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]], Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha-jinja Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan.<ref name="Mainichi20201121" />

Key area:
* Front section width: 129.5 meters
* Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters
* Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front)

<gallery>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the east</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view of the front section</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the rear</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the west</small>
File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flanged cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]]
</gallery>

== Geography ==
The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} spans the northernmost edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konabe Kofun|qq=}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment.

== Overview ==
The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the burial site of [[:en:Princess Yata|Princess Yata]], daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and consort of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].

== burial and area ==
The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]. The first tier was surrounded by a row of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus.

Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]], Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha-jinja Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan.<ref name="Mainichi20201121" />

Key area:
* Front section width: 129.5 meters
* Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters
* Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front)

<gallery>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the east</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view of the front section</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the rear</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the west</small>
File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flanged cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]]
</gallery>

== Geography ==
The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} spans the northernmost edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konabe Kofun|qq=}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment.

== Geography ==
The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} spans the northernmost edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konabe Kofun|qq=}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment.

== Overview ==
The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the burial site of [[:en:Princess Yata|Princess Yata]], daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and consort of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].

== burial and area ==
The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]. The first tier was surrounded by a row of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus.

Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]], Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha-jinja Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan.<ref name="Mainichi20201121" />

Key area:
* Front section width: 129.5 meters
* Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters
* Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front)

</gallery>

== Geography ==
The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} spans the northernmost edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konabe Kofun|qq=}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment.

== Overview ==
The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the burial site of [[:en:Princess Yata|Princess Yata]], daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and consort of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].

== burial and area ==
The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]. The first tier was surrounded by a row of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus.

Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]], Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha-jinja Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan.<ref name="Mainichi20201121" />

Key area:
* Front section width: 129.5 meters
* Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters
* Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front)

<gallery>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the east</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view of the front section</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the rear</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the west</small>
File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flanged cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]]
</gallery>

== Geography ==
The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} spans the northernmost edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konabe Kofun|qq=}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment.

== Overview ==
The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the burial site of [[:en:Princess Yata|Princess Yata]], daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and consort of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].

== burial and area ==
The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]. The first tier was surrounded by a row of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus.

Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]], Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha-jinja Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan.<ref name="Mainichi20201121" />

Key area:
* Front section width: 129.5 meters
* Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters
* Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front)

<gallery>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the east</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view of the front section</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the rear</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the west</small>
File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flanged cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]]
</gallery>

== Geography ==
The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} spans the northernmost edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konabe Kofun|qq=}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment.

The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]. The first tier was surrounded by a row of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus.

Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]], Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha-jinja Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan.<ref name="Mainichi20201121" />

Key area:
* Front section width: 129.5 meters
* Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters
* Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front)

<gallery>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the east</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view of the front section</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the rear</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the west</small>
File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flanged cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]]
</gallery>

== Geography ==
The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} spans the northernmost edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konabe Kofun|qq=}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment.

== Overview ==
The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the burial site of [[:en:Princess Yata|Princess Yata]], daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and consort of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].

== burial and area ==
The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]. The first tier was surrounded by a row of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus.

Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]], Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha-jinja Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan.<ref name="Mainichi20201121" />

Key area:
* Front section width: 129.5 meters
* Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters
* Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front)

It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konabe Kofun|qq=}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}}. == Overview ==
The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the burial site of [[:en:Princess Yata|Princess Yata]], daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and consort of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].

== burial and area ==
The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]. The first tier was surrounded by a row of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus.

Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]], Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha-jinja Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan.<ref name="Mainichi20201121" />

Key area:
* Front section width: 129.5 meters
* Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters
* Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front)

<gallery>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the east</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view of the front section</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the rear</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the west</small>
File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flanged cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]]
</gallery>

== Geography ==
The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} spans the northernmost edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konabe Kofun|qq=}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment.

== Overview ==
The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the burial site of [[:en:Princess Yata|Princess Yata]], daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and consort of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].

== burial and area ==
The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]. The first tier was surrounded by a row of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus.

Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]], Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha-jinja Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan.<ref name="Mainichi20201121" />

Key area:
* Front section width: 129.5 meters
* Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters
* Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front)

<gallery>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the east</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view of the front section</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the rear</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the west</small>
File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flanged cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]]
</gallery>

== Geography ==
The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} spans the northernmost edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konabe Kofun|qq=}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment.
