== Overview ==
The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the burial site of [[:en:Princess Yata|Princess Yata]], daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and consort of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].

== burial and area ==
The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]. The first tier was surrounded by a row of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus.

Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]], Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha-jinja Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan.<ref name="Mainichi20201121" />

Key area:
* Front section width: 129.5 meters
* Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters
* Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front)

<gallery>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the east</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view of the front section</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the rear</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the west</small>
File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flanged cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]]
</gallery>

== Geography ==
The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} spans the northernmost edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konabe Kofun|qq=}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment.

== Overview ==
The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata|en|Princess Yata|WD=Q10892311|lt=Princess Yata}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}.

== burial and area ==
The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus.

Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha-jinja Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan.<ref name="Mainichi20201121" />

Key area:
* Front section width: 129.5 meters
* Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters
* Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front)

<gallery>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the east</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view of the front section</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the rear</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the west</small>
File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flanged cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, displayed at the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute}}
</gallery>

== Geography ==
The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konabe Kofun|qq=}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment.

== Overview ==
The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata|en|Princess Yata|WD=Q10892311|lt=Princess Yata}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}.

== burial and area ==
The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus.

Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha-jinja Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan.<ref name="Mainichi20201121" />

Key area:
* Front section width: 129.5 meters
* Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters
* Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front)

<gallery>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the east</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view of the front section</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the rear</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the west</small>
File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flanged cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, displayed at the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute}}
</gallery>

== Geography ==
The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konabe Kofun|qq=}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment.

the largest in Saki Kofun Cluster|work=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-11-21|access-date=2023-12-12}}</ref>, Height 19.8 meters | built = Mid-{{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} | burials = {{ill|Princess Yata|en|Princess Yata|WD=Q10892311|lt=Princess Hata|1=Princess Yata}} (proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}) | burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Uwanabe burial Reference Site" | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} | image = File:Uwanabe Kofun, funkyu.jpg | notes = Ranked 12th or 13th in Japan depending on length (270–280 meters or 255 meters)<ref name="Mainichi20201121" /><ref>[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Kofun size Rankings (National)] from the Sakai City website, last updated 2019-11-14, accessed 2020-12-13.</ref> | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=41|coordinates=51.45|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=32.47|map_dot_label=Uwanabe Kofun|mark=Zenpō-kōen-fun.svg|marksize=23|caption=location within Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|51.45|N|135|48|32.47|E}}}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }}
== Overview == The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata|en|Princess Yata|WD=Q10892311|lt=Princess Yata|1=Princess Yata}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}.
== burial and area == The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus.
Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha-jinja Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan.<ref name="Mainichi20201121" />
Key area: * Front section width: 129.5 meters * Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters * Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front)
<gallery> File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the east</small> File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view of the front section</small> File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the rear</small> File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the west</small> File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flanged cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, displayed at the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} </gallery>
== Geography == The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konabe Kofun|qq=}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment.

== Geography ==
The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konabe Kofun|qq=}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment.

== Overview ==
The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata|en|Princess Yata|WD=Q10892311|lt=Princess Yata|1=Princess Yata}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}.

== burial and area ==
The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus.

Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha-jinja Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan.<ref name="Mainichi20201121" />

Key area:
* Front section width: 129.5 meters
* Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters
* Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front)

File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the west</small>

</gallery>

== Geography ==
The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konabe Kofun|qq=}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment.

== Overview ==
The '''Uwanabe Kofun''' (宇和奈邊古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is the easternmost and largest tumulus in the group. built in the mid-5th century, it is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} as the burial site of {{ill|Princess Yata|en|Princess Yata|WD=Q10892311|lt=Princess Yata|1=Princess Yata}}, daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} and consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}.

== burial and area ==
The tumulus is a three-tiered structure covered with {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}}. The first tier was surrounded by a row of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}}. While the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency, the surrounding moat is maintained by local farmers. Vegetation now covers the surface of the tumulus.

Earlier surveys estimated its length at 255 meters, but a 2020 excavation by the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}}, Nara City Board of Education, and the Imperial Household Agency found the tumulus to be 270-280 meters long, surpassing the {{ill|Gosha-jinja Kofun|ja|五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|2=ja|3=五社神古墳|4=fr|5=Tombe de Gosashi|6=zh|7=五社神古墳|1=Gosashi Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha-jinja Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosashi Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (267 meters) within the same cluster. This makes it the 12th-largest tumulus in Japan.<ref name="Mainichi20201121" />

Key area:
* Front section width: 129.5 meters
* Rear circular section diameter: 128 meters
* Height: 19.8 meters (rear), 16 meters (front)

<gallery>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 2 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the east</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 3 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view of the front section</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 4 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the rear</small>
File:CG Uwanabe Kofun 1 7pbw.jpg|<small>3D rendering, view from the west</small>
File:ウワナベ古墳出土 鰭付円筒埴輪.JPG|{{ill|Flanged cylindrical haniwa|ja|鰭付円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flanged cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, displayed at the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}}
</gallery>

== Geography ==
The {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} spans the northernmost edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}. It includes the {{ill|Konabe Kofun|ja|コナベ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konabe Kofun|qq=}} and other notable Kofuns such as {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are surrounded by moats, giving them the appearance of islands within a wetland environment.
